wins: iter-3 (drift-cancelled A/B vs iter-2 control: +0.27% headline, +1.14% B=16): per-workload SPLIT_MAX routing on host-available avg_kv_len. Long-KV B=16 workloads boosted +27% (1e15ed03) / +20% (ccdc67b6).
dead_ends: iter-1 (-3.5% headline): SPLIT_MAX=8 globally for B=16 — reduce kernel's per-CTA partial-O load doubles (4 slots→8) for 14/16 short-KV workloads where no boost is needed; inflation > long-KV gain.

iter-4 (-5.55% headline / -24% B=16): short-KV-B=16 (avg_kv_len<400) routed to fused — per-batch kv_len variance WITHIN short-KV workloads (e.g. 0ea2f83b: 129..466) makes max-CTA dominate fused wall time. fused-B=16 dead at every routing granularity short of per-batch dispatch.
iter-5 (-3.22% headline): reduce kernel num_warps 2→4 — small-kernel per-warp overhead doesn't amortize at 4 warps for a 2-4KB-load kernel.
ncu_profile: Same as round-2 parent (kernel structure unchanged on B=64; SPLIT_MAX=4 path matches parent exactly). NCU on iter-3 reduce: 9.92% occupancy, Est. Local Speedup 97.24% — packed reduce remains an untried lever.
variance_note: Session noise was unusually high — B=16 CV 19% vs round-2's 0.9%; cross-session absolute score 1.30 < parent 1.44 but A/B-anchored long-KV gain is the structural signal.
